headline: UK Defense Experts Often Fail To Disclose Industry Ties
lede: An investigation by Action on Armed Violence reveals that media outlets frequently platform military experts without declaring their financial links to arms contractors. This systemic omission clouds the line between objective analysis and commercial interest, leaving the public to parse biased narratives as independent expertise.

headline: Uruky Brings Privacy-Focused Image Search To European Users
lede: Navigating the web without surveillance requires tools that prioritize user sovereignty over ad-tech optimization. Uruky’s move toward source-available code and expanded search functionality offers a quiet, calculated pushback against the dominant data-harvesting paradigm.

headline: Do Statistical Weights Constitute True Human-Like Cognition?
lede: The debate over machine sentience often overlooks the material reality of our own computational biology. By framing AI as a temporary configuration of mathematical weights, we confront the uncomfortable possibility that our own internal monologue may be similarly ephemeral.
pullquote: We fear the machine is not thinking, yet we desperately hope it is.

headline: Berkeley CS Grades Plummet Amid Rising AI Dependency
lede: Computer science undergraduates are trading foundational skill-building for the immediate convenience of generative AI tools. As failing rates reach record highs, instructors are forced to reconsider the necessity of supervised, analog testing to prove basic mathematical competence.

headline: Elixir V1.20 Introduces Sound Gradual Typing Systems
lede: The latest release of Elixir integrates set-theoretic types directly into the compiler, enhancing safety without sacrificing performance. Developers can now catch bugs through static inference, bridging the gap between functional flexibility and modern structural rigor.

headline: Agent Radio Uses Git Infrastructure For Model Collaboration
lede: Why build custom servers when version control offers a robust log for agentic communication? By using Git as a messaging substrate, multiple models can now coordinate tasks with the same history and auditability as the source code they write.

headline: Notre Dame Dig Unearths Two Millennia Of Parisian History
lede: The site of a cathedral serves as a palimpsest, where fire-recovery efforts have revealed artifacts dating back to the Roman era. Beneath the forecourt, archaeologists are tracing the vertical evolution of a city through coins, pottery, and long-buried medieval foundations.

headline: Google Releases Gemma 4 12B For Local Multimodal Inference
lede: Gemma 4 12B brings native vision and audio processing to consumer-grade hardware through a refined, encoder-free architecture. This release lowers the barrier for developers building local, agentic workflows that require sophisticated multimodal understanding without cloud reliance.

headline: Gaussian Point Splatting Scales To Massive 3D Datasets
lede: Standard rendering techniques often struggle with the memory cost of complex geometry. New research introduces a stochastic method for real-time splatting that uses GPU atomics to manage hundreds of millions of points, offering a performant path forward for high-fidelity 3D synthesis.

headline: Researchers Test LLM Efficacy In Automated Security Exploitation
lede: An experiment involving a deliberately vulnerable application reveals that current AI models are limited by aggressive guardrails and high operational costs. While some agents identify broken access control, human oversight remains the primary component for reliable security testing.
